The exercise found uneven implementation across firms and jurisdictions, prompting ESMA to issue interim supervisory expectations while acknowledging that the broader sustainable finance regulatory framework is actively being revised.

initiative: DLT collateral mobility platform HQLAᵡ gets SEC no action letter for US activity
description: HQLAᵡ, the DLT collateral mobility platform, can operate in the US under a no action letter received from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Eurex Clearing recently leveraged the platform to accept DLT-based collateral for margin purposes, one of the first instances worldwide at a traditional regulated venue. The key benefit is enabling instant transfer rather than waiting for T+1 or T+2 settlement. The SEC letter was addressed to both HQLAᵡ and Clearstream International, which acts as the trusted third party and collateral custodian. Underlying assets are locked in place and digital collateral receipts are issued that can be transferred instantly. HQLAᵡ is backed by Deutsche Börse and major banks including BNP Paribas, BNY, Citi, Goldman Sachs, HSBC, and JP Morgan. The platform currently runs on R3 Corda but plans to migrate to Canton technology. HQLAᵡ is also applying for a permanent conditional exemption from clearing agency registration; the no action letter provides 36 months of breathing space.

initiative: 21Shares lists Strategy-linked STRC ETN on London Stock Exchange, expands UK footprint
description: 21Shares has launched its Strategy Yield ETN (ticker: STRC) on the London Stock Exchange, giving UK retail and institutional investors their first exchange-traded access to Strategy Inc.'s Variable Rate Series A Perpetual Preferred Stock — a preferred equity instrument linked to the company's bitcoin treasury operations. The underlying security currently yields 11.50% paid monthly in cash, and Strategy has indicated it may selectively sell bitcoin holdings to fund dividend obligations, a notable shift from its prior "never sell" posture disclosed during Q1 earnings when the firm posted a $12.5 billion net loss largely from mark-to-market bitcoin adjustments.

initiative: Morgan Stanley brings crypto trading with lower fees than rivals
description: Morgan Stanley has launched a crypto trading pilot on its E*Trade platform at 50 basis points per transaction, undercutting established retail competitors — Coinbase, Robinhood, and Charles Schwab — which charge between 60 and 95 basis points, with a planned full rollout to all 8.6 million E*Trade customers later in 2026. The offering sits within a broader infrastructure build-out that includes a pending national trust bank charter for direct digital asset custody, in-kind crypto-to-ETF conversion capabilities, and preparatory work for tokenized equity trading.

initiative: Blockchain.com launches SnapMarkets as prediction markets move into faster crypto bets
description: The launch occurs as prediction markets surge, facing regulatory conflicts between the pro-market CFTC and challenging state gambling authorities.

initiative: From 'what' to 'how': Robinhood and Bitstamp say banks are ready to build on-chain
description: Executives from Ondo, Robinhood and Babylon Labs said at Consensus Miami 2026 that banks and traditional finance firms are embracing crypto infrastructure, though institutional adoption remains constrained.

initiative: Banks reject US stablecoin compromise, public shrugs at crypto
description: Crypto firms are pushing a stablecoin compromise to revive stalled U.S. legislation, but banks resist, while the public remains indifferent.

initiative: RootData maps 30 Hyperliquid Web3 partners as it builds an on-chain liquidity OS
description: RootData’s new map shows 30 core Web3 partners plugging custody, trading, wallets, and infra into Hyperliquid’s L1 as it pushes toward an on-chain liquidity OS. RootData’s latest ecosystem map shows Hyperliquid positioning itself as a performant L1 “optimized from the ground up”…

initiative: Monetary policy transmission to exchange rates: the role of currency carry trades
description: Researchers at the Bank for International Settlements published a bulletin examining how currency carry trades — in which leveraged investors such as hedge funds borrow in low-interest-rate funding currencies to invest in higher-yielding ones — interact with and amplify the exchange rate effects of monetary policy decisions. The study finds that when carry traders have built up significant short positions in funding currencies ahead of a policy announcement, a tightening move triggers accelerated unwinding of those leveraged positions, producing an outsized exchange rate response beyond what standard transmission mechanisms would predict.

initiative: Bitcoin approaches $82,000 as oil crashes 6% on fresh Iran peace deal hopes
description: Axios reported that Washington and Tehran are nearing a one-page memorandum of understanding brokered through U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with Iran reportedly agreeing to remove highly enriched uranium from the country — a concession it had previously refused. The prospect of de-escalation in the Strait of Hormuz, which Iranian forces have reportedly mined since late February, drove WTI crude futures down roughly 6% to $95.28 a barrel, relieving the supply-disruption premium that has weighed on energy-dependent Asian markets in particular. The simultaneous bid in risk assets — Nasdaq futures up over 1%, Bitcoin pushing toward $82,000 — reflects a sharp repositioning out of geopolitical hedges and into growth-sensitive exposures.

initiative: Anchorage Digital launches AI banking for autonomous payments
description: Crypto bank Anchorage Digital has introduced an agentic banking service that allows AI systems to access and move funds across traditional and crypto payment rails without human intervention. According to Anchorage Digital co-founder and CEO Nathan McCauley, the new infrastructure…

initiative: Crypto custodian Taurus moves straight into EU capital markets with MiFID license in Cyprus
description: Taurus, the Swiss crypto custody and tokenization firm, has received a MiFID II investment services license from the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC), granting it passporting rights to offer regulated investment services across all EU member states. The license authorizes the firm to act as a broker-dealer for tokenized transferable securities — including bonds, equities, fund shares, and structured products — as well as DLT-based financial instruments that fall within the MiFID instrument perimeter, directly targeting the institutional client base of banks and asset managers.

initiative: Kelp DAO Claims LayerZero Approved the Setup It Blamed for $292 Million Hack, Migrates to Chainlink
description: Aakash Girimath Posted May 6, 2026 at 4:46 am EST. Kelp DAO published a detailed memo on Wednesday claiming that LayerZero personnel directly approved the bridge configuration that LayerZero subsequently blamed for the $292 million rsETH exploit . Kelp DAO Claims LayerZero Approved the Setup It Blamed for $292 Million Hack, Migrates to Chainlink Unchained

initiative: Kraken Partners With MoneyGram for Global Crypto-to-Cash Withdrawals, Says IPO Is “80% Ready”
description: Kraken and MoneyGram have formalized a phased global distribution agreement enabling Kraken users to withdraw cryptocurrency as cash at approximately 500,000 MoneyGram retail agent locations across more than 100 countries, with support for hundreds of fiat currencies. The operational division of labor is clearly delineated: Kraken retains control of customer onboarding and KYC/AML obligations, while MoneyGram supplies the licensed money transmission infrastructure and regulatory coverage in each jurisdiction.

initiative: Coinbase Cuts 700 Jobs and Restructures Around AI as Crypto Downturn Deepens
description: Coinbase Workforce Reduction and AI Restructuring (May 2026) Coinbase eliminated approximately 700 positions — 14% of its total workforce — effective immediately, following a Q4 2025 net loss of $667 million on revenue that declined 21.6% quarter-over-quarter, with Bitcoin having shed more than a third of its value from its October 2025 peak near $126,000. The company is guiding for $50–60 million in restructuring charges concentrated in Q2 2026, ahead of its Q1 earnings release scheduled for Thursday.

initiative: Stablecoins enter AI payments as Solana and Google launch Pay.sh
description: Solana and Google Cloud launched Pay.sh, letting AI agents pay for Google Cloud and other APIs using stablecoins on Solana.

initiative: CFTC may lock in Phantom-style crypto wallet protections
description: CFTC Chair Brian Selig, speaking at Consensus Miami on May 6, signaled the agency intends to convert its March 17 no-action letter issued to Phantom Technologies into formal rulemaking, potentially extending broker registration exemptions to the broader non-custodial wallet developer market. The original Phantom letter allowed the Solana-based self-custody wallet provider to facilitate user access to registered futures commission merchants, introducing brokers, and designated contract markets without itself registering as an introducing broker or associated person, provided it never held customer funds.

initiative: Report on Vulnerabilities in Private Credit
description: Private credit has expanded rapidly to an estimated $1.5–2 trillion in assets, supporting financing for mid-sized companies but creating potential financial stability risks

initiative: UK sets new rules for fund tokenization under current regulations
description: The UK's Financial Conduct Authority published policy statement PS26/7 on April 30, formalising how asset managers can deploy distributed ledger technology within the existing regulatory framework — without waiting for bespoke legislation. Two structural mechanisms are now explicitly sanctioned: a "Direct to Fund" model in which investors deal directly with the fund or its depositary rather than through the manager, enabling atomic on-chain settlement of unit issuances and cancellations in a single step; and the industry-developed "Blueprint" model, under which an on-chain transaction record can serve as the primary register, removing the mandatory parallel off-chain mirror provided adequate resiliency controls exist.

initiative: Coinbase sued by anonymous whale over frozen DAI from $55M scam
description: A large crypto holder identified only as "D.B." filed a federal lawsuit against Coinbase after the exchange froze a portion of approximately $55 million in DAI stolen from his wallet on August 20, 2024, via an Inferno Drainer phishing attack that exploited a spoofed DeFi Saver login page. Blockchain security firm Zero Shadow traced a portion of the stolen DAI to a Coinbase retail account, and the exchange did freeze the assets upon notification — a step the plaintiff's counsel acknowledged as appropriate — but Coinbase subsequently declined to release the funds back to D.B. even after he provided sworn attestations of ownership.

initiative: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse says CLARITY Act could stall before midterms
description: Speaking at the Consensus conference, Ripple's CEO warned that the CLARITY Act — the digital asset market structure bill that has already cleared the House and a Senate Agriculture Committee markup — faces a closing window of roughly two weeks before midterm election dynamics make it politically toxic to advance. His comments represent a meaningful downward revision from his own prior forecasts of 80% April passage and a Memorial Day signing.

initiative: Tennessee bankers pick Stablecore as digital asset push grows
description: Tennessee Bankers Association picked Stablecore to help member banks offer stablecoins, tokenized deposits and crypto-backed lending tools.

initiative: Cybersecurity strains grow as AI challenges Australian banks
description: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) issued a formal letter to regulated financial institutions — banks, insurers, and superannuation trustees — warning that existing cybersecurity frameworks are failing to keep pace with rapid advances in frontier AI models. APRA specifically identified advanced-coding-capable systems, citing Anthropic's Claude Mythos as an example of technology that could enable malicious actors to detect and exploit infrastructure vulnerabilities at scale. The regulator's supervisory review found that boards and senior leadership lack sufficient technical literacy to govern AI-related risks, and that heavy reliance on third-party AI vendors is creating material blind spots around potential misuse or manipulation of those tools.

initiative: PayPal Plans Job Cuts
description: PayPal is executing a significant workforce reduction under incoming CEO Enrique Lores, with the company's roughly 23,800-person headcount implying a potential elimination of more than 4,500 positions if cuts reach the ~19% range reported. The restructuring is tied to a formal commitment to deliver at least $1.5 billion in cost savings over the next two to three years, representing a material strategic pivot away from the innovation-led agenda articulated by predecessor Alex Chriss. The move comes alongside a separate reported exploration of spinning Venmo out as a standalone legal entity, suggesting leadership is conducting a broad structural review of which business lines to retain, divest, or reposition.

initiative: Crypto whale sues Coinbase for allegedly refusing to return stolen DAI
description: In August 2024, an anonymous high-net-worth investor known as "D.B." lost approximately $55 million in DAI after a phishing attack leveraging the "Inferno Drainer" toolkit granted an attacker access to his wallet. Blockchain security firm Zero Shadow traced a portion of the stolen funds to a Coinbase retail user account, prompting D.B. to notify the exchange, which subsequently froze the assets. Coinbase declined to release the frozen funds back to the plaintiff absent a court order establishing ownership — a position D.B.'s legal team characterizes as unreasonable given sworn attestations of rightful ownership already provided to the exchange.

initiative: Kelp DAO moves rsETH to Chainlink CCIP as LayerZero dispute grows
description: Kelp DAO announced it will migrate its rsETH liquid restaking token from LayerZero's OFT standard to Chainlink's Cross-Chain Token standard under CCIP, following an April 18 exploit in which attackers drained 116,500 rsETH—worth approximately $292 million—from its LayerZero-powered bridge by exploiting a single-DVN (decentralized verifier network) configuration. The two protocols are publicly disputing responsibility for that configuration: Kelp contends LayerZero's team was aware of and implicitly endorsed the 1-of-1 verifier setup during integration discussions dating to January 2024, while LayerZero CEO Bryan Pellegrino asserts Kelp itself downgraded from a default multi-DVN arrangement to the weaker single-verifier setup that LayerZero does not recommend for production.

initiative: Coinbase Centrifuge
description: Announcements May 5, 2026 Coinbase and Centrifuge are collaborating to accelerate tokenization and onchain finance Coinbase has designated Centrifuge as a Preferred Tokenization Infrastructure platform, supporting the next phase of tokenized asset growth through utility and onchain access.

initiative: The world's entire economy will be tokenized, says Consensys’ Joseph Lubin
description: The Consensys CEO and founder said tokenization can be traced back to Ethereum, the blockchain he helped co-found.

initiative: It's transparency, not tech alone, that drives crypto adoption, panelists tell Consensus Miami
description: At CoinDesk's Consensus Miami conference on May 5, senior product and finance executives from PayPal, Robinhood, Public.com, and 248 Ventures collectively argued that the primary constraint on retail crypto and AI-product uptake is user trust architecture rather than technical capability. Robinhood VP Nicola White disclosed that half of the platform's Q1 new users were first-time investors, using that data point to question whether 100x-leverage perpetuals should be available to retail customers at all — a direct operational critique of current industry product velocity following the October 2026 crypto liquidation event.

initiative: Different voices in product, policy and hiring change crypto outcomes, panelists tell Consensus Miami
description: Senior leaders from Mastercard, the Crypto Council for Innovation and Clerisy said the right people in the right rooms can reshape internal decisions, citing examples from stablecoin-linked cards and financial access to staking-policy framing in Washington.

initiative: Kraken eyes IPO as it partners with MoneyGram to bridge crypto-to-cash gap
description: Kraken's parent company Payward has structured a distribution agreement with MoneyGram that connects the exchange's crypto and stablecoin infrastructure to MoneyGram's approximately 500,000 physical retail locations globally, targeting the conversion friction between digital assets and local-currency cash that limits adoption in markets such as Latin America where banking penetration remains low. The arrangement is operationally significant because MoneyGram was taken private in 2023 and has been rebuilding its business around digital rails, making Kraken a strategic channel partner rather than a legacy remittance competitor.

initiative: State Street says institutions want improved blockchain security in wake of recent DeFi attacks
description: Angus Fletcher, State Street’s head of digital assets said the young crypto industry needs to find solutions now before trillions in RWAs come on-chain.

initiative: Self-directed investors power bitcoin ETF launch despite Morgan Stanley’s scale
description: Morgan Stanley's spot bitcoin ETF (MSBT) gathered over $200 million in assets within its first few weeks, a notably fast pace for a traditional ETF launch, but the inflows were driven almost entirely by self-directed retail clients making autonomous allocation decisions rather than through the firm's financial advisor channel. Amy Oldenburg, Morgan Stanley's newly appointed head of digital assets, disclosed at Consensus Miami that a meaningful portion of demand is coming from existing spot crypto holders migrating assets from self-custodied wallets into regulated exchange-traded products, signaling a structural shift in how crypto-native investors interface with traditional financial infrastructure.

initiative: CFTC looks to cement non-custodial software developer protections following Phantom no-action letter
description: CFTC Moves to Formalize Non-Custodial Developer Exemptions via Rulemaking CFTC Chair Michael Selig announced at Consensus Miami that the agency intends to convert its March 2026 no-action relief granted to Phantom wallet into binding rules, establishing a permanent regulatory safe harbor for non-custodial software developers who meet specified conditions and do not hold customer assets. The March no-action letter had already signaled that such developers would not be required to register as introducing brokers under the Commodity Exchange Act, but Selig indicated a preference for codified rulemaking over ad hoc guidance to provide durable legal certainty.

initiative: Citi exec says fragmented crypto systems risk repeating old banking problems
description: At the Consensus Miami 2026 conference, Citi's head of digital assets for treasury and trade solutions, Ryan Rugg, laid out a pointed operational critique of the current tokenized finance landscape: bank-siloed tokenization platforms cannot meet the cross-institutional, real-time payment demands of large corporate clients that routinely manage hundreds or thousands of accounts across multiple global banks. Rugg noted that Citi has already built an internal tokenized deposit platform connected to a 24/7 USD clearing network spanning more than 300 institutions, but stressed this is insufficient on its own given the broader industry's lack of common standards and interoperability.

initiative: Overseas demand for U.S. equities is growing, says Robinhood's Johan Kerbrart
description: The trading app's senior vice president said tokenization, 24-hour trading, and regulatory shifts are driving broader investor access beyond domestic markets.

initiative: Securitize taps Jump as market maker for trading tokenized stocks on ATS
description: Securitize, the company best known for tokenizing BlackRock’s BUIDL money market fund, is gradually announcing the building blocks of its tokenized stock and trading strategy. The latest announcement is the addition of Jump Trading as liquidity provider for its trading venue, the Securitize Markets alternative trading system (ATS), plus a collaboration with Jupiter to provide

initiative: Kelp DAO ditches LayerZero for Chainlink’s cross-chain infrastructure following $292 million exploit
description: Following the April 18 exploit in which Lazarus Group-linked attackers drained 116,500 rsETH (~$292 million) by abusing a single-verifier LayerZero bridge configuration, Kelp DAO has migrated its cross-chain infrastructure to Chainlink's Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) and adopted the Cross-Chain Token (CCT) standard for rsETH. The attack exposed a structural weakness in LayerZero's default 1-of-1 Decentralized Verifier Network setup — a configuration later found to be running across roughly 47% of ~2,665 LayerZero applications — with the two parties publicly disputing whether that single-verifier default constituted a platform-level failure or a protocol-specific misconfiguration.

initiative: CME Group to launch cash-settled bitcoin volatility futures
description: CME Group announced it will list Bitcoin Volatility futures (ticker: BVI) on June 1, 2026, pending CFTC regulatory approval, making it the first such product available on a regulated U.S. exchange. The cash-settled contracts will carry a notional multiplier of $500 times the CME CF Bitcoin Volatility Index (BVX), which measures 30-day implied volatility derived in real time from CME's existing CFTC-regulated Bitcoin and Bitcoin Micro options order books — infrastructure that CF Benchmarks and CME jointly formalized in December 2024.

initiative: GoMining unveils GoBTC payments protocol with 0.2% merchant fee
description: GoMining’s GoBTC protocol promises instant authorization and on-chain Bitcoin settlement with a 0.2% merchant fee, positioning miner-run rails as a low-cost challenger to Visa and Mastercard. According to Forbes, GoMining will formally debut its GoBTC payment protocol at this year’s Consensus…

initiative: Kelp claims that LayerZero approved the setup it blamed for $292 million bridge hack
description: Read full story Latest Crypto News Bitcoin mining pools with 75% of BTC hashrate join open standard for block construction 56 minutes ago XRP spikes 2.5%, beating bitcoin and ether, in breakout above $1.45 1 hour ago A bitcoin whale that went silent in 2013 moves $40 million in BTC 2 hours ago Policy at Consensus Miami: State of Crypto 12 hours ago Agentic commerce will run on crypto rails, PayPal and Google reps tell Consensus Miami 16 hours ago Bored Ape NFTs are finally making a comeback as crypto traders rediscover their appetite for risk 17 hours ago Top Stories BlackRock deepens tokenization push with new onchain fund offerings May 9, 2026 Crypto industry cheers Senate Clarity Act markup date as market structure push resumes May 9, 2026 Senate Banking Committee plans to hold key

initiative: Crypto ETPs log five straight weeks of inflows, topping $4B
description: CoinShares' weekly flow report shows global crypto ETP products recorded $117.8 million in net inflows for the most recent week, but the aggregate figure obscures a pronounced intraweek dislocation: Monday through Thursday generated $619 million in net outflows before a single-day $737 million surge on Friday rescued the weekly balance. Bitcoin-linked products, predominantly U.S. spot ETFs operating under the SEC's post-approval regulatory framework, absorbed $192.1 million in net inflows, while Ethereum products shed $81.6 million as traders unwound ETH exposure despite that asset class having posted strong inflows in prior weeks. Regionally, U.S.

initiative: Tokenization won't disrupt banking rails but improve them, Wall Street executives say
description: At the Consensus 2026 conference in Miami, senior digital asset executives from Citigroup, JPMorgan, and DTCC provided concrete production metrics signaling that institutional tokenization has moved decisively past the pilot stage: Citi's tokenized deposit platform has scaled from millions to billions of dollars processed, while JPMorgan's Kinexys blockchain platform has cleared over $1 trillion in cumulative transactions. DTCC, which sits at the center of U.S. post-trade infrastructure handling roughly $150 trillion in securities annually, confirmed active rollout plans to migrate portions of that stack onto a shared digital layer, underscoring the systemic significance of the transition.

initiative: SoFi to launch its stablecoin on Solana, citing speed and cost
description: SoFiUSD is a fully reserved U.S. dollar stablecoin issued by SoFi Bank, a nationally chartered bank.

initiative: Bullish acquisition of Equiniti targets tokenized securities
description: Crypto exchange Bullish has agreed to acquire Equiniti, a UK-based transfer agent, for $4.2 billion, in a deal announced May 5, 2026. Equiniti currently maintains shareholder registries for approximately 3,000 major corporations and 20 million individual shareholders, giving Bullish immediate operational custody of the ownership records, legal registrations, and corporate action history that tokenized equity issuance requires at institutional grade. The transaction follows material regulatory developments enabling the deal's commercial logic, including the SEC's approval of Nasdaq's tokenized stock trading trial and Federal Reserve guidance on bank treatment of tokenized securities, both issued in early 2026.

initiative: Banking groups reject Clarity Act yield compromise
description: Major US banking associations rejected the Clarity Act’s stablecoin yield compromise, splitting publicly from Coinbase and Circle Major US banking associations publicly rejected the stablecoin yield compromise brokered by Senators Tillis and Alsobrooks in the Clarity Act. The banking groups…

initiative: Western Union’s Solana-based stablecoin could reshape its payment model, analyst says
description: Western Union launched USDPT, a dollar-backed stablecoin on Solana, issued by Anchorage Digital Bank, for 24/7 settlement with agents and partners.

initiative: State Street and Galaxy launch fund on Solana designed to ‘sweep’ stablecoins into productive vehicle
description: The State Street Galaxy Onchain Liquidity Sweep Fund lets investors push stablecoins into a yield-bearing tokenized fund.

initiative: MoonPay acquires Solana execution layer firm DFlow used by Coinbase, Phantom
description: MoonPay, primarily known as a fiat-to-crypto on-ramp, has completed the acquisition of DFlow, a Solana-based order routing and execution infrastructure provider, in an all-stock transaction reported at approximately $100 million. DFlow had already established itself as embedded trading infrastructure for major counterparties including Coinbase and Phantom, processing roughly 10 million transactions per month and over $50 billion in cumulative volume across Solana. The deal extends MoonPay's addressable surface beyond retail conversion flows into the execution layer, positioning it to serve high-frequency trading pipelines and programmatic or AI-agent-driven transaction flows.

initiative: Garlinghouse defends Clarity Act Senate momentum
description: Speaking at Consensus 2026 in Miami before an audience that included SEC Chair Paul Atkins and CFTC Chair Brian Selig, Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse publicly backed the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, citing measurable growth in Senate support as evidence the bill is advancing beyond lobbying rhetoric. The Clarity Act has drawn coordinated endorsements from Coinbase and Circle, who jointly urged the Senate Banking Committee to advance the legislation after Senators Tillis and Alsobrooks brokered a compromise on stablecoin yield provisions. Major US banking associations have filed opposition to those same yield provisions, contending they introduce systemic risk to incumbent financial institutions, creating a direct lobbying standoff that will likely define committee markup timing.

initiative: Ripple to share DPRK hacker intel with crypto industry after $577M in DeFi hacks
description: Ripple is feeding North Korea–linked threat intelligence into Crypto ISAC, hoping shared context on DPRK operatives and DeFi exploits can blunt a 2026 hack wave led by Drift and KelpDAO. Summary Ripple is contributing exclusive North Korea–linked threat intelligence to the Crypto ISAC information-sharing platform, arguing that “the strongest security posture in crypto is a shared one.” DPRK hackers have stolen about $577 million in crypto so far in 2026—76% of all hack losses year-to-date—largely via two DeFi exploits on Drift Protocol and KelpDAO. The intelligence covers enriched profiles of suspected North Korean IT operatives and detailed indicators of compromise (IOCs), as attackers pivot from pure technical exploits to long, social engineering–driven campaigns.

initiative: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse says Clarity better than chaos as Senate hits key moment
description: Speaking at Consensus 2026 in Miami, Ripple's CEO identified a narrow two-week window as determinative for the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, which is pending a Senate Banking Committee markup hearing before it can advance to a full floor vote. Negotiators recently released compromise language on stablecoin yield — drawing a line between permissible crypto rewards programs and bank-style interest-bearing deposit accounts — that was intended to unblock the committee scheduling, though a coalition of banking trade groups publicly rejected the compromise as insufficient this week.

initiative: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse warns next two weeks are critical for crypto legislation
description: With the House having already passed its version of broad digital asset market structure legislation (the "Clarity Act") last year, the Senate remains the critical bottleneck, requiring passage out of both the Agriculture and Banking committees before a bill can reach the president's desk. The Agriculture Committee has cleared its version, but the Banking Committee has stalled over disagreements on stablecoin yield treatment—a logjam that Senators Alsobrooks and Tillis partially addressed with a compromise reached last week, potentially enabling a markup vote this month.

initiative: MoneyGram supports crypto-to-cash withdrawals for Kraken users
description: The firms plan to expand this partnership beyond cash withdrawals to support local bank deposits and cross-border remittance flows.

initiative: Google Cloud, Solana Foundation roll out new pay-as-you-go system for AI agents
description: The Solana Foundation and Google Cloud jointly launched Pay.sh, an open payments infrastructure that allows AI agents to access and pay for API services—including Google Cloud's own BigQuery, Gemini, and Cloud Run—using stablecoins on Solana, without requiring account creation or subscriptions. The system operates via an API proxy hosted on Google Cloud Platform that authenticates agent requests and routes payments through either Coinbase's x402 protocol or Stripe/Tempo's Machine Payments Protocol, giving it interoperability with two competing emerging standards for autonomous machine commerce.

initiative: Santiago Bausili: Finance, economics and investments
description: Santiago Bausili, President of the Banco Central de la República Argentina (BCRA), delivered a formal policy address at Expo EFI on 29 April 2026, providing a comprehensive operational update on Argentina's stabilization program now in its third year following its late-2023 launch. He reported that the BCRA had accumulated USD 6.9 billion in foreign reserve purchases year-to-date, materially exceeding market forecasts, and that the central bank had permitted over USD 1.3 billion in dividend repatriations by foreign corporates—the first such outflows permitted in six years—as exchange restrictions continue to be unwound in stages.

initiative: Sanjay Malhotra: Indian financial markets - resilience and resurgence
description: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra delivered the keynote address at the 25th FIMMDA-PDAI Annual Conference in Amsterdam on 1 May 2025, laying out both the current state of Indian financial markets and a forward-looking reform agenda directed at primary dealers, banks, and foreign portfolio investors. He outlined a series of concrete regulatory developments already in force, including the extension of central clearing for FX forwards to 36-month tenors (up from 13 months), the introduction of initial margin requirements for non-centrally cleared derivatives via CCIL infrastructure, new electronic trading platforms for forex options and Modified MIFOR derivatives, and the connection of NDS-OM to global bond trading platforms to deepen secondary G-sec liquidity.

initiative: Aleš Michl: Forever hawkish
description: Czech National Bank Governor Aleš Michl delivered a guest lecture at MIT Sloan School of Management on 30 April 2026, outlining the CNB's policy trajectory since he assumed the governorship in July 2022 amid 17.5% inflation that subsequently peaked at 18%. He detailed how a near-complete turnover of the seven-member Bank Board, driven by expiring term limits rather than political dismissal, allowed the new leadership to pursue a sustained disinflationary strategy despite inheriting a key rate already at 7%. The CNB reached its 2% inflation target in February 2024, ahead of most European peers, with inflation averaging 2.4–2.5% in the two years since.

initiative: Michelle W Bowman: Artificial intelligence in the financial system
description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman used a FSOC AI roundtable appearance to announce two concrete regulatory actions: the Fed, OCC, and FDIC jointly amended model risk management guidance (SR 26-2, April 17, 2026) to explicitly exclude generative and agentic AI from its scope, and the Fed is separately revising its third-party risk management guidance to reduce ambiguity around vendor AI relationships. A specific near-term threat driving urgency is Anthropic's "Mythos" model—a tool capable of autonomously identifying cyber vulnerabilities—which prompted Treasury Secretary Bessent and Fed Chair Powell to convene the largest U.S. banks for an emergency briefing on its dual-use implications.

initiative: Chiara Scotti: Digital money and the architecture of trust
description: Bank of Italy Deputy Governor Chiara Scotti delivered the welcome address at a joint workshop on digital assets and monetary policy transmission, co-organised by the Bank of Italy, the ECB, the Euro Area Business Cycle Network, and CEPR in Rome on 4 May 2026. Her remarks framed the central policy challenge not as a technical comparison of CBDCs, stablecoins, and tokenised deposits in isolation, but as a question of institutional architecture—specifically, what combination of settlement assets, institutions, and infrastructure can preserve trust, monetary control, and systemic resilience alongside financial innovation.

initiative: Piero Cipollone: Digital assets, payment efficiency and monetary policy
description: ECB Executive Board member Piero Cipollone used a joint ECB/Bank of Italy/CEPR workshop in Rome to formally articulate the Eurosystem's operational framework for integrating tokenisation into the monetary system, announcing that from September 2026 the Eurosystem will offer tokenised central bank money settlement for DLT-based transactions through its Pontes project. The ECB also confirmed it has already begun accepting DLT-issued marketable assets as eligible collateral for Eurosystem credit operations since late March, with further eligibility expansion under active consideration for assets not represented in conventional securities settlement systems.

initiative: Crypto's value is from being outside regulatory apparatus, says Arthur Hayes
description: Speaking at Consensus Miami 2026, Arthur Hayes — co-founder of BitMEX and CIO of the crypto-focused family office Maelstrom — argued that legislative and regulatory integration efforts such as the Senate's Clarity Act are structurally misaligned with bitcoin's core value driver, which he identifies solely as the rate of global fiat money creation. Drawing on a cross-administration price history, Hayes pointed to specific monetary expansion events — post-2008 bank bailouts, COVID stimulus, and Ukraine-driven commodity inflation — as the operative catalysts behind bitcoin's appreciation, rather than any policy legitimacy conferred by regulatory frameworks. His remarks arrive at a moment of active U.S.

initiative: Jito Labs launches self-custody trading tool as activity heats up on Solana
description: JTX is the company’s first product built specifically for traders. It allows users to trade tokens on Solana while maintaining self-custody.

initiative: Coinbase taps Centrifuge as preferred tokenization backbone, takes equity stake
description: The partnership includes a strategic investment by Coinbase and is focused on expanding tokenized ETFs, credit, and structured products.

initiative: State Street and Galaxy launch tokenized fund to bring cash management onchain
description: The fund lets institutions earn yield on stablecoins while moving cash onchain with round-the-clock access.

initiative: K Wave Media scraps massive bitcoin treasury plan to redirect $485 million to AI
description: K Wave Media (KWM), a Nasdaq-listed Korean media and entertainment company, filed with the SEC to amend its June 2025 structured equity facility with Anson Funds, redirecting up to $485 million in remaining capacity away from bitcoin purchases and into AI infrastructure including data centers, GPU compute, and acquisitions across the AI value chain. The original $500 million facility had been established explicitly to accumulate bitcoin as a treasury asset, a strategy that gained capital markets traction in 2025 when such announcements reliably lifted share prices.

initiative: StanChart’s SC Ventures invests in crypto market maker GSR
description: Standard Chartered's venture and innovation arm SC Ventures has taken a strategic minority stake in GSR, a London-headquartered crypto market maker and OTC trading desk, marking the first outside strategic investment in the firm and valuing it at approximately $1 billion. The deal deepens an already active relationship between the two parties, as GSR recently led the funding round for Libeara, the tokenization platform incubated by SC Ventures. GSR, which reported $287 million in revenue and $71 million in after-tax profit for the year ending June 2024, had previously raised $167 million in total funding, with UK-registered CNC Inversiones holding a controlling interest following a 2021 investment.

initiative: Securitize taps Jump and Jupiter as it rolls out fully onchain, regulated onchain stocks
description: Jump will provide institutional liquidity via its PropAMM, while Solana DeFi aggregator Jupiter will support retail and institutional access.

initiative: Coinbase doubles down on Centrifuge investment, taps platform as Base’s main tokenization partner
description: Centrifuge will be the go-to platform for launching tokenized assets on Base, the Ethereum blockchain incubated by Coinbase.

initiative: Galaxy and State Street launch Solana-based SWEEP tokenized cash fund
description: stocks opened higher on Tuesday, with major indexes gaining and AI‑linked storage names outperforming, even as PayPal sank about 10% after disappointing profit guidance. According to CoinDesk, State Street and Galaxy have rolled out SWEEP, a “tokenized private liquidity fund”…

initiative: Standard Chartered expands further into crypto with stake in GSR at $1 billion valuation
description: SC Ventures, the venture capital arm of Standard Chartered, has taken a minority equity stake in GSR — a crypto market-making and liquidity firm founded in 2013 by former Goldman Sachs traders — at a valuation exceeding $1 billion, representing the first external investment in GSR's thirteen-year history. The deal is structured as a broader commercial partnership, with the two firms intending to co-develop regulated market infrastructure connecting traditional banking rails to digital asset markets, with a particular focus on tokenization services for institutional clients.

initiative: Coinbase cuts 14% of staff as AI reshapes how crypto companies operate
description: Coinbase announced the elimination of approximately 660 roles — roughly 14% of its 4,700-person workforce — with CEO Brian Armstrong attributing the decision to both a sustained crypto market downturn and a structural shift in engineering productivity driven by AI tooling that has materially reduced headcount requirements for software development. The move is part of a broader cost restructuring at the Nasdaq-listed exchange intended to right-size the business through the current down-market cycle rather than carry overhead built for more favorable conditions.

initiative: Global crypto ETP inflow streak extends to five weeks, masking sharp intraweek reversal: CoinShares
description: CoinShares' weekly flow report showed global crypto exchange-traded products attracted $117.8 million in net inflows for the week ending May 2, extending a consecutive positive-flow streak to five weeks and accumulating over $4 billion in that period, though the headline figure conceals significant underlying stress. Products run by issuers including BlackRock, Fidelity, Grayscale, Bitwise, and 21Shares experienced four straight days of outflows totalling $619 million Monday through Thursday, before a single Friday session reversed the trajectory with $737 million in inflows — one of the largest single-day figures recorded in 2026.

initiative: Coinbase to cut 14% of workforce as Armstrong pushes AI-native structure
description: Coinbase announced on May 5, 2026, that it is eliminating approximately 14% of its global workforce, with affected employees losing system access the same day as a security precaution given the firm's custodial obligations. CEO Brian Armstrong framed the reduction as a response to both cyclical softness in crypto markets and a structural reorganization around AI-driven workflows, citing the ability of smaller, automated teams to compress development timelines significantly. The restructuring includes flattening the organization to a maximum of five management layers below the CEO and COO, eliminating pure management roles in favor of player-coach positions, and piloting AI-native pods where single individuals may span product, design, and engineering functions.

initiative: Tether begins first stablecoin audit as Lutnick loan scrutiny mounts
description: Tether posts strong Q1 profits as its long-awaited audit begins, while Senate scrutiny, controversial loans, and global stablecoin regulations intensify.

initiative: Bank of Italy calls for EU to explore tokenized SEPA payments
description: Bank of Italy's Chiara Scotti said the EU should explore a tokenized extension of its payments system as tokenization grows more "relevant."

initiative: Elon Musk settles SEC Twitter case with $1.5M fine
description: The SEC's civil enforcement action against Elon Musk—stemming from his failure to timely file a Schedule 13G disclosure after acquiring more than 5% of Twitter's shares in early 2022—has been resolved via a $1.5 million penalty paid by a Musk-controlled trust, with no admission of wrongdoing and subject to federal court approval in Washington, D.C. The regulator's core allegation was that Musk filed the required beneficial ownership disclosure 11 days past the statutory deadline, enabling him to continue accumulating shares at pre-disclosure prices and allegedly avoiding approximately $150 million in additional acquisition costs—none of which he is required to disgorge under the settlement terms.

initiative: Bullish bets $4.2B on tokenized securities with Equiniti deal
description: Bullish agreed to buy Equiniti for $4.2B, aiming to offer issuer tokenization, 24/7 securities trading and stablecoin settlement.

initiative: ESMA consults on a new simplified approach to updating MMF stress test parameters
description: ESMA has opened a public consultation proposing to replace the existing practice of annually amending Section 5 of its MMF stress test Guidelines with a streamlined web-based publication of updated calibration parameters each year. Under the proposed structure, the Guidelines themselves would remain static as the governing framework and methodology, while a dedicated ESMA webpage would serve as the live, authoritative source for current calibration inputs — allowing funds to apply updates immediately upon approval rather than waiting for a formal guidelines amendment cycle.

initiative: Ripple shares North Korea-linked threat data as crypto attacks shift to social engineering
description: Ripple has contributed its proprietary threat intelligence — including fraud-associated domains, wallet addresses, and indicators of compromise tied to North Korean hacking campaigns — to Crypto ISAC, a sector-focused information-sharing nonprofit, which simultaneously launched a new API enabling real-time data distribution among members including Coinbase. The disclosure is framed around a documented tactical shift by North Korea-linked actors away from on-chain exploits toward insider-style social engineering, as illustrated by the $280 million Drift incident, where attackers gained the trust of contributors and compromised their devices rather than targeting smart contract vulnerabilities.

initiative: US spot bitcoin ETFs draw $532 million as inflows extend to a third day, led by BlackRock and Fidelity
description: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs Register $532M in Net Inflows on Third Consecutive Positive Day On May 4, 2026, the 13 SEC-registered spot bitcoin ETFs collectively absorbed $532.2 million in net capital, bringing the three-day running total to $1.18 billion and lifting cumulative net inflows across the product category to $59.3 billion since launch. BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC dominated activity, contributing $335.5 million and $184.6 million respectively, with Morgan Stanley's MSBT adding a further $12.2 million — the remaining ten funds, including Grayscale's GBTC, reported flat flows and zero outflows.

initiative: SEC Pumps the Brakes on First Prediction-Market ETFs, Delaying 24 Products
description: The SEC intervened just before the automatic 75-day effectiveness deadline to halt the pending launches of 24 prediction-market ETFs filed in February by Roundhill Investments, GraniteShares, and Bitwise, requesting supplemental information on product structuring and investor risk disclosure. The proposed funds would have provided retail brokerage access to binary event contracts traded on CFTC-regulated venues such as Kalshi, covering macro and political outcomes, without requiring investors to hold accounts on those platforms directly. Bloomberg's ETF desk characterizes the hold as procedurally narrow—focused on pricing mechanics and real-time probability transparency—rather than a substantive rejection of the asset class.

initiative: Ripple’s David Schwartz reveals he is nearly all in on XRP
description: Ripple CTO Emeritus David Schwartz disclosed on May 5, 2026, that his personal crypto holdings have been reduced to almost exclusively XRP and Ripple equity, with negligible exposure to any other digital asset. Speaking in an online discussion, he characterized the concentration as largely unplanned rather than a deliberate strategic allocation, which is notable given his decades-long technical leadership at the firm that issues XRP. Schwartz simultaneously argued that broad sector diversification remains a rational approach for investors who lack conviction on which specific assets will win — a framing that separates his own insider-adjacent position from a general endorsement of XRP concentration.

initiative: Ripple begins sharing DPRK threat intel with crypto firms
description: Ripple has formalized the sharing of its internal threat intelligence on North Korean state-linked actors — including wallet addresses, domains, indicators of compromise, and personal identifiers such as LinkedIn profiles and email addresses — through Crypto ISAC's newly updated API, which standardizes data feeds across Web2 and Web3 security systems. The move is a direct operational response to a documented tactical shift by groups like Lazarus, away from smart contract exploits toward long-term insider infiltration, as demonstrated in the Drift incident where attackers cleared hiring processes, built internal credibility over months, and ultimately compromised multisig wallets without triggering code-level alerts.

initiative: Coinbase opens crypto access for Australia’s self-managed retirement funds
description: Coinbase Australia has activated dedicated account support for self-managed superannuation funds (SMSFs) under its Australian Financial Services Licence, secured in April 2026, which subjects the firm to the same conduct, disclosure, and consumer protection obligations as traditional financial intermediaries. The product includes ATO-aligned downloadable transaction data and a verification workflow tailored to the trustee structure of SMSFs — addressing a key operational friction point for the roughly 664,000 funds collectively holding AU$1.06 trillion in assets. The move places Coinbase in direct competition with OKX, which launched a comparable SMSF-focused crypto platform in September 2025 featuring custody, compliance tooling, and end-of-year reporting.

initiative: The impact of stablecoins on the international monetary and financial system
description: Widespread use of stablecoins could have an important impact on the international monetary and financial system, particularly for emerging market and developing economies (EMDEs). Using the framework of international currency functions to analyse their effect, we argue that stablecoins are most likely to affect private sector store of value and medium of exchange roles, particularly in economies facing macroeconomic

initiative: Coinbase Australia launches crypto support for self-directed retirement funds
description: Coinbase Australia has formally extended its platform to serve self-managed superannuation fund (SMSF) trustees, a move enabled by the Australian Financial Services Licence the exchange secured just weeks prior. The offering includes ATO-aligned accounting data exports and an entity verification workflow adapted to SMSF legal structures, addressing the specific compliance and audit requirements that have historically made institutional-grade crypto access difficult for this vehicle. SMSFs are regulated directly by the Australian Taxation Office and collectively held approximately AU$1.06 trillion across roughly 664,000 funds at end-2025, representing a substantial addressable pool of retirement capital that is already legally permitted to hold crypto.

initiative: Strive pushes past 15,000 BTC with fresh $33.9M bitcoin buy
description: Strive, Inc. disclosed via an SEC 8-K filing that it acquired 444 BTC for approximately $33.9 million at an average cost of $76,307 per coin, bringing its total treasury to just over 15,000 BTC valued at roughly $1.2 billion at current prices. The purchase continues a structured accumulation program that has added more than 2,200 BTC since the Dallas-based firm absorbed Semler Scientific as a subsidiary in January 2026. Strive's capital strategy relies heavily on its SATA-ticker perpetual preferred stock, which raised $225 million against more than $600 million in demand and carries a ~13% annualized yield, with proceeds channeled directly into bitcoin purchases.

initiative: Moscow Exchange bets bigger on crypto with SOL, XRP, TRX and BNB indexes
description: Moscow Exchange (MOEX) will begin publishing four new cryptocurrency reference indexes — MOEXSOL, MOEXXRP, MOEXTRX, and MOEXBNB — from May 13, 2026, restricted to qualified (professional) investors under Russia's tiered access framework for digital asset products. Pricing will be sourced from four offshore exchanges, with Binance carrying a dominant 50% weight and Bybit, OKX, and Bitget supplying the remainder, reflecting the absence of domestic regulated spot crypto liquidity. Simultaneously, MOEX will increase the calculation frequency of its existing Bitcoin and Ethereum indexes to every 15 seconds, including weekend sessions, bringing benchmark granularity closer to the standard required for derivative underliers.

initiative: Uphold rejects NYAG claims after $5M CredEarn settlement
description: Uphold disputes NYAG claims after a $5M CredEarn settlement, saying Cred misled the company and users before its 2020 collapse.

initiative: Bank groups challenge Senator Thom Tillis over stablecoin proposal
description: banking groups have pushed back against the latest CLARITY Act language on stablecoin rewards, arguing it does not sufficiently prevent risks to bank deposits. According to a joint statement from the American Bankers Association, Bank Policy Institute, Consumer Bankers…

initiative: Ripple to share North Korean threat intelligence with crypto firms
description: Ripple said April's $285 million Drift breach revealed a new pattern of long-cycle social engineering replacing traditional smart contract exploits.

initiative: Stablecoins to hit $5T in B2B payments by 2035: Juniper Research
description: New research sees cross-border B2B stablecoin flows hitting $5T by 2035, reaching 85% share as enterprise use and 24/7 settlement drive payments shift.

initiative: Brazil's Central Bank Tightens Control Over Digital Payments
description: The Banco Central do Brasil enacted two concurrent measures targeting its digital payments infrastructure. First, it imposed new transaction caps on Pix transfers initiated from unregistered devices—limiting individual transfers to R$200 (approximately $35) and daily flows to R$1,000—while granting major retail banks including Nubank, Itaú Unibanco, and Caixa Econômica Federal authority to place precautionary holds of up to 72 hours on flagged transactions. Second, through Resolution No. 561, the central bank banned FinTechs and eFX providers from using stablecoins or cryptocurrencies as back-end settlement rails for cross-border payments, effective October 1, 2026, while leaving retail crypto ownership untouched.

initiative: DTCC lines up 50 giants for tokenized securities launch
description: DTCC plans July tokenized securities pilots and an October launch with 50 firms, covering ETFs, Russell 1000 stocks and U.S. Treasuries.

initiative: Crypto stocks rally amid Clarity Act momentum; Circle jumps nearly 20%
description: Sens. Alsobrooks and Tillis finalized a compromise Friday on language governing stablecoin yields in the Clarity Act.

initiative: Bank trade groups say Senate stablecoin reward fix ‘falls short’ amid deposit protection concerns
description: Major banking trade groups said a proposed fix to a key sticking point in passing crypto market structure legislation 'falls short.'

initiative: Chainlink price spikes 3% at Consensus 2026
description: LINK posted its largest single-session advance in two weeks on May 4, breaking out of a month-long consolidation range between roughly $8.70 and $9.58, coinciding with the opening of the Consensus 2026 conference in Miami and Bitcoin reclaiming the $80,000 level in a broad risk-on session. The move was preceded by a notable exchange outflow signal, with Santiment data recording 970,430 LINK tokens leaving centralized venues on April 27—the highest single-day outflow since December 2025—suggesting accumulation pressure had been building ahead of the catalyst.

initiative: Why Morgan Stanley says Bitcoin on US bank balance sheets is coming but not yet
description: Morgan Stanley's head of digital asset strategy, Amy Oldenburg, told attendees at the Bitcoin 2026 Conference in Las Vegas that direct Bitcoin holdings on U.S. bank balance sheets remain blocked by two specific regulatory barriers: the Basel Committee's 1,250% risk-weight for unbacked crypto assets, which makes proprietary exposure economically impractical under current capital rules, and the absence of a Federal Reserve examiner framework governing Bitcoin exposure. The Basel Committee indicated in February 2026 that it had accelerated a targeted review of its crypto capital standards, which Oldenburg cited as meaningful progress over the past 16 months.

initiative: Ripple Custody signs South Korea’s first insurance deal to pilot near real-time bond settlement
description: Ripple Custody entered a strategic partnership with Kyobo Life Insurance on April 15, making Ripple Custody Korea’s first blockchain-based government bond settlement platform for a Tier 1 insurer, targeting a compression of the standard T+2 settlement cycle to near real-time…

initiative: Coinbase to open BILL-USD spot trading for Billions token
description: According to Coinbase’s latest listing update, the exchange “will launch BILL (Billions) spot trading” and expects to open the BILL-USD order book later today in supported trading regions, contingent on sufficient liquidity… BILL-USD pair to launch once liquidity is ready.

initiative: Latest FINRA okay means Securitize becomes tokenized securities one stop shop

description: District Court for the District of Colorado, Payward Interactive and Payward Trading — doing business as Kraken — accuse Etana Custody Limited, Etana Custody Inc., CEO Dion Brandon Russell, and others… In a second amended complaint submitted to the U.S.

initiative: Brian Armstrong tells the Senate to “mark it up” after CLARITY Act stablecoin yield deal lands
description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong posted a three-word response on X on May 1 after Senators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks released the final stablecoin yield compromise text for the CLARITY Act: “Mark it up,” urging the Senate Banking Committee to… Brian Armstrong tells the Senate to “mark it up” after CLARITY Act stablecoin yield deal lands

initiative: Hut 8 swaps Coinbase loan for cheaper FalconX deal, slashing borrowing costs as it bets big on AI
description: Hut 8 has replaced its existing Coinbase Credit bitcoin-backed lending arrangement with a new $200 million, 364-day facility provided by crypto prime broker FalconX, reducing its fixed interest rate from 9% to 7% — a 200-basis-point improvement that directly lowers the company's cost of capital. A material operational consequence of the refinancing is the release of approximately 3,300 BTC (~$260 million) that had been locked as pledged collateral under the prior facility's covenants, restoring the company's ability to deploy or leverage that bitcoin more flexibly.

description: Upbit is the Optimism Foundation's first client on its "self-managed" enterprise tier, giving it control over its core rollup architecture.

description: The market is starting to price in potential winners as stablecoin yield compromise opens path for passing key U.S. digital asset regulation, one analyst said.

initiative: Shopify and National Bank of Canada are among backers of a new digital currency built to settle trades 24/7
description: Tetra Trust's new stablecoin, CADD, is aimed at institutional use for 24/7 cross-border settlement, real-time corporate treasury, and direct fintech transfers, replacing legacy batch systems.

initiative: DTC to begin tokenized asset rollout in July ahead of full launch as it gets feedback from BlackRock, Circle and others
description: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation will roll out its tokenization service as it gathers feedback from firms like BlackRock to Circle.

initiative: SEC pumps the brakes on prediction market ETFs set to debut this week: Reuters
description: The SEC intervened to halt the launch of 24 prediction market ETFs from issuers including Bitwise, Roundhill, and GraniteShares that had been progressing through a 75-day expedited review window established under last year's fast-track rules. The products were designed to offer binary, yes-or-no exposure to outcomes such as the 2028 U.S. election result, tech-sector layoffs, and recession probability — structures that differ fundamentally from conventional ETFs in that investors could lose substantially all capital if the underlying outcome resolves against them.

initiative: Wall Street giant DTCC plans tokenized securities platform with July pilot, October launch
description: Clearing giant's move toward live tokenization market adds weight to Wall Street's blockchain push.

initiative: Western Union launches USDPT stablecoin issued by Anchorage on Solana
description: Western Union launched a dollar stablecoin on Solana for 24/7 settlement across 200+ countries, the company said Monday.

description: Etana is accused of operating a "Ponzi-like enterprise" the resulted in using capital to fund expenses and risky investments.

description: Rain, which was valued at $1.95 billion during a Series C round announced in January, has an existing relationship with card issuer Visa.

description: ESMA advances the simplification of EU reporting frameworks for funds and transactions 04 May 2026 Fund Management Market data Press Releases Securities Financing Transactions Simplification and Burden Reduction The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA), the EU financial markets regulator and supervisor, has launched a harmonised approach to funds reporting and has set a clear path towards streamlined

initiative: Kraken's parent company Payward alleges $25 million crypto custody fraud in lawsuit against Etana and firm's CEO
description: Payward filed a second amended complaint in U.S. District Court in Colorado alleging that Etana Custody — a Wyoming-based firm that served as Kraken's fiat on-ramp partner and held customer reserve funds — misappropriated over $25 million by commingling client assets with operating expenses and speculative investments, including at least $16 million deployed into promissory notes issued by Seabury Trade Capital that subsequently defaulted. When Kraken attempted to withdraw approximately $25 million in April 2025, Etana allegedly stalled with fabricated reconciliation explanations while using incoming client deposits to cover existing shortfalls — a structure the complaint characterizes as Ponzi-like.

initiative: Brazil imposes partial ban on stablecoins, crypto for cross border payments and FX
description: Brazil’s central bank has banned fintech and payment provider cross border services from using stablecoins or crypto to settle with overseas counterparties. Resolution BCB 561, published last Thursday and effective from October 2026, states that settlement between an eFX provider and its overseas counterparty must occur exclusively through traditional FX operations or non-resident BRL accounts.

initiative: Bitget CFD daily volume hits record $8B on gold surge
description: Bitget's contracts-for-difference platform registered a single-day notional volume of $8 billion, up from a prior milestone of $6 billion reached in March 2026, with precious metals contracts — primarily XAUUSD — accounting for approximately 95% of the incremental flow as traders sought macro hedges amid geopolitical risk and inflation-driven rate uncertainty. The exchange operates these CFDs under a USDT-margined "UEX" model that allows users to cross-collateralize positions across crypto derivatives and traditional asset classes — commodities, FX, and equity indices — from a single account, removing the fiat on-ramp friction typical of regulated brokerages.

initiative: Kraken parent Payward closes $550 million Bitnomial deal, securing full CFTC derivatives stack
description: Payward completed its acquisition of Bitnomial for up to $550 million in cash and stock, gaining direct ownership of Bitnomial's CFTC-licensed futures exchange, futures commission merchant, and derivatives clearinghouse — a vertically integrated regulatory stack that eliminates dependence on third-party venues for U.S. derivatives execution and clearing. The deal follows Payward's 2025 acquisition of NinjaTrader and positions the combined entity to launch spot margin trading first, with perpetual futures and options to follow as product approvals progress. Beyond retail-facing products, Payward intends to offer B2B access through Payward Services, enabling banks, fintechs, and brokerages to connect to CFTC-regulated crypto derivatives through a single integration point.

initiative: Rain adds Mastercard support as $1.95B stablecoin startup targets on-chain settlement
description: Rain, a $1.95B-valued stablecoin infrastructure firm, is adding Mastercard support to extend its Visa-based model and plug on-chain settlement into legacy payment systems. Summary Stablecoin infrastructure firm Rain, valued at $1.95 billion, has secured a partnership with Mastercard to issue credit and prepaid cards and to pilot stablecoin-based settlement flows for institutional clients. The deal marks Rain’s move from a Visa-only setup to a dual-network model, aimed at letting large enterprises add stablecoin settlement “without changing their existing payment systems.” The tie-up comes as players like Stripe and Coinbase roll out their own stablecoin payment rails, accelerating the convergence of traditional finance and crypto settlement.

initiative: Western Union brings USDPT to Solana in global payments push
description: Western Union launched USDPT on Solana with Anchorage issuing the stablecoin, while SOL traded near $86 as traders watch $90 resistance now.

initiative: Korean crypto exchanges push back against strict 10M won AML trigger
description: South Korea's Digital Asset Exchange Alliance (DAXA), representing 27 registered virtual asset service providers, has filed formal objections with the Financial Services Commission and the Financial Intelligence Unit against a proposed amendment to the Specific Financial Information Act's enforcement decree that would require exchanges to automatically file suspicious transaction reports on any cross-border crypto transfer exceeding 10 million won (approximately $6,800), irrespective of actual risk indicators. DAXA estimates the rule would cause annual STR filings across the five major platforms — Upbit, Bithumb, Coinone, Korbit, and Gopax — to surge 85-fold from roughly 63,000 to over 5.4 million, effectively collapsing the signal-to-noise ratio that AML regimes depend on to function.

initiative: Bitget and WIW3CH expand Blockchain4Her for women in Web3
description: Bitget has entered a strategic partnership with women-focused Web3 collective WIW3CH to deliver more structured education, networking, and career support under its Blockchain4Her initiative. Bitget said it has formed a strategic alliance with WIW3CH, describing the group as a Web3…

initiative: Prediction markets under fire as insiders game the system
description: A U.S. Army Special Forces soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, was charged by both the DOJ and CFTC on April 23 after allegedly exploiting advance knowledge of the U.S. military operation that captured Venezuelan former president Nicolás Maduro to place over $33,000 in bets on Polymarket, generating nearly $410,000 in winnings paid out in USDC. In response, Polymarket engaged Chainalysis to monitor for market integrity violations — a reactive compliance measure signaling that the platform's existing controls were insufficient to detect or prevent insider exploitation.

initiative: Hut 8 unlocks $260M in Bitcoin as it refinances $200M FalconX loan
description: Hut 8's subsidiary has entered a new $200 million Bitcoin-collateralized credit agreement with FalconX, fully retiring an equivalent facility previously held with Coinbase Credit and reducing the fixed annual interest rate from 9% to 7% — a 200 basis point improvement that directly lowers the company's cost of non-dilutive debt. The refinancing triggers the release of roughly 3,300 BTC from pledged collateral to unencumbered status, materially improving balance sheet flexibility at a time when mining margins are compressed and treasury management is central to miner competitiveness. FalconX, which has structured similar Bitcoin-backed facilities with traditional counterparties including Cantor, brings an institutional prime brokerage framework to the arrangement.

initiative: Coinbase boosts Solana trading with DFlow integration
description: Coinbase has designated DFlow as its primary liquidity routing protocol for Solana-based spot and prediction market trading, replacing an infrastructure stack that previously failed to route approximately one in every 30 trades due to insufficient liquidity coverage. Under the new arrangement, that failure rate drops to roughly one in 250, while tokens that previously returned no executable sell-side quotes are now routable through DFlow's aggregation logic, which sources paths that competing aggregators do not surface. DFlow already processes order flow for over one million active traders monthly and was previously integrated by prediction market operator Kalshi in December 2025, giving it an established track record in high-throughput, latency-sensitive environments.

initiative: Senators Release CLARITY Act Stablecoin Yield Compromise as White House Pushes for May Markup
description: The bipartisan compromise bans passive stablecoin yield but allows activity-based rewards, clearing the final major hurdle to a Senate Banking Committee markup on the market structure bill.

initiative: Acting under uncertainty - the case for supervisory risk appetite frameworks
description: The Bank for International Settlements' Financial Stability Institute published a working paper proposing a formal supervisory risk appetite framework (RAF) for banking regulators, addressing the longstanding absence of structured tools for supervisors to define and communicate their own tolerance for failing to meet prudential objectives. The paper, authored by Monica Balan and Raihan Zamil, argues that without such a framework, supervisory decisions become inconsistent and poorly timed, particularly during periods of banking stress when intervention credibility matters most.

initiative: Anthropic lines up $1.5B AI venture with Blackstone, Goldman Sachs
description: Anthropic is close to finalizing a roughly $1.5 billion joint venture with Blackstone, Goldman Sachs, and several other Wall Street firms to distribute artificial intelligence tools to private-equity-backed companies. A report by The Wall Street Journal said the platform will…

initiative: Morgan Stanley advises 2%–4% Bitcoin exposure as demand grows
description: Morgan Stanley's head of digital asset strategy, Amy Oldenburg, publicly outlined a 2%–4% Bitcoin portfolio allocation recommendation for clients during the Bitcoin Conference in Las Vegas, framing it as a response to growing client interest in regulated crypto access rather than speculative positioning. The bank's Bitcoin ETP, MSBT, validated that demand early, pulling in over $100 million within six days of launch entirely from self-directed clients before the product was even made available through Morgan Stanley's advisory channel — highlighting a structural lag between retail initiative and adviser-driven distribution.

initiative: Kraken parent Payward completes Bitnomial acquisition, unlocking US crypto derivatives offering
description: Payward has closed its acquisition of Bitnomial, a Chicago-based crypto-native exchange, consolidating ownership of three CFTC-issued licenses — a Futures Commission Merchant, a Designated Contract Market, and a Derivatives Clearing Organization — into a single regulated derivatives stack under the Kraken umbrella. The combined entity can now offer spot margin, perpetuals, and options to eligible U.S. clients across Kraken and NinjaTrader, with spot margin slated for rollout first. Bitnomial will continue operating under its existing regulatory framework and retain its third-party business relationships, preserving license integrity while being absorbed into Payward's infrastructure.

initiative: Kraken unlocks full U.S. derivatives play after Bitnomial buy
description: Payward, Kraken's parent company, has closed its acquisition of Chicago-based Bitnomial, gaining simultaneous control of three CFTC licenses — a Futures Commission Merchant, a Designated Contract Market, and a Derivatives Clearing Organization — a combination that no other crypto-native U.S. firm currently holds together. The deal, previously disclosed at up to $550 million in cash and stock, gives Payward a vertically integrated regulatory structure covering trading, clearing, and brokerage under a single framework. Bitnomial will retain its existing regulatory standing and third-party relationships while its infrastructure is connected across Kraken, retail futures platform NinjaTrader, and a B2B API layer targeting banks, brokerages, and payment firms.

initiative: Coinbase urges CFTC to keep prediction markets under rules
description: Coinbase submitted a formal letter on April 30 to CFTC Secretary Christopher Kirkpatrick in response to the agency's Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, arguing that prediction market products already fall within the CFTC's existing statutory authority and require no new legislative mandate to regulate. Chief Policy Officer Faryar Shirzad framed event-based contracts as functionally analogous to traditional futures instruments, both serving as mechanisms for aggregating dispersed information into price signals, and called for a principles-based oversight framework prioritizing market integrity.

initiative: CFTC prediction market rules spark industry debate
description: The CFTC's comment period closed on a March 12 proposed rulemaking that would formalize federal oversight of prediction market event contracts under Part 38 of the Commodity Exchange Act, drawing over 1,500 submissions from a notably divided set of stakeholders. Major platform operators Kalshi and Polymarket, alongside venture firm Andreessen Horowitz, submitted letters explicitly backing CFTC's exclusive jurisdiction, arguing that fragmented state-level intervention would undermine compliance frameworks already built around federal oversight.

initiative: Mastercard’s AI agent Payment in the Netherlands
description: Mastercard and Rabobank jointly executed what is described as the Netherlands' first AI agent-initiated payment, using Mastercard's Agent Pay framework to allow a consumer-facing AI assistant to book a coffee tasting experience through Mastercard's Priceless.com platform, charged to a Rabobank-issued Mastercard credit card. The transaction was triggered by a natural-language consumer prompt, with the agent completing the purchase autonomously without ever accessing or transmitting raw card credentials. Agent Pay enforces tokenization and passkey-based authentication, meaning the agent operates within pre-configured consent boundaries set by the cardholder, which is a meaningful trust and liability distinction for issuers and acquirers navigating emerging agentic commerce.

initiative: Morgan Stanley's Oldenburg: Bitcoin on U.S. bank balance sheets is coming, just not yet
description: Morgan Stanley's head of digital asset strategy, Amy Oldenburg, publicly acknowledged that U.S. bank balance sheet holdings of bitcoin are a foreseeable outcome but flagged a multi-layered regulatory gauntlet — Federal Reserve oversight, Basel III/IV capital treatment, and coordination across global supervisors — as the substantive barriers preventing near-term action at a firm of Morgan Stanley's systemic scale. Her comments came alongside disclosure that the bank's newly launched MSBT bitcoin ETP, custodied jointly by Coinbase and BNY Mellon, gathered over $100 million in its first six days exclusively through self-directed retail channels, before the product was even made available to the firm's own financial advisor network.

initiative: Americans still prefer banks over crypto for financial access, CoinDesk's survey shows
description: A survey of 1,000 randomly selected U.S. voters conducted by Public Opinion Strategies found that 65% trust banks over crypto for financial access, with only 5% favouring digital assets, and 60% viewing crypto as a mostly negative economic force — figures that arrive as the industry is lobbying Congress to pass the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act against organised opposition from the banking sector. The timing is operationally significant because banks have been blocking that legislation by arguing that stablecoin yield products would compete directly with interest-bearing deposits and redirect funding away from U.S. lending activity.

initiative: New York AG settles for $5 million with Uphold in first enforcement action against a crypto yield product promoter
description: The settlement is the first New York enforcement action to target a platform that promoted someone else's crypto yield product rather than its own issuer.

initiative: Crypto market recap: What happened today?
description: Senate negotiators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks reached a compromise on the stablecoin rewards provision that had been blocking the CLARITY Act, with Coinbase's Chief Policy Officer confirming that crypto platforms retained the right to offer rewards tied to genuine network and platform activity, while bank-preferred restrictions on deposit-like yield were accepted. Separately, the NYSE filed a proposed rule change with the SEC to enable tokenized securities trading under DTC's three-year pilot program, requiring tokenized instruments to retain the same CUSIP, ticker, and shareholder rights as their underlying shares and settle via DTC on a T+1 basis.

initiative: NYSE moves closer to tokenized stocks under DTC pilot
description: NYSE filed a rule change to allow tokenized equities and ETFs under DTC’s pilot, keeping the same rights and T+1 settlement process.

initiative: Coinbase says crypto bill deal clears Senate path
description: Coinbase says a Senate deal on stablecoin rewards may clear the CLARITY Act’s path to markup after months of bank and crypto disputes today.

initiative: BlackRock pushes OCC to rethink tokenized reserve limits
description: BlackRock urged the OCC to drop a tokenized reserve cap and widen eligible stablecoin assets as BUIDL adoption grows in crypto markets fast.

initiative: Iran crypto giant Nobitex hit by sanctions questions: Reuters
description: A Reuters investigation revealed that Nobitex — which claims 11 million users and processes roughly 70% of Iran's domestic crypto volume — was founded in 2018 by Ali and Mohammad Kharrazi, members of a politically connected Iranian clerical family, operating under the alternative surname Aghamir.
